note: Sprint 5.18d hotfix: makes `sost-cli sendrawtransaction <hex>` wallet-free. The first VPS broadcast attempt failed with `Error loading wallet 'wallet.json': cannot open wallet.json` because the subcommand was dispatched AFTER the global wallet load. The subcommand handler is now positioned BEFORE the wallet load in `int main()`, right after the `newwallet` short-circuit, so the wrapper never opens, parses or even references a wallet file. It remains a pure RPC wrapper (rpc_call("sendrawtransaction", ...)) and still respects global RPC flags --rpc-user/--rpc-pass/--node which are parsed earlier in main(). All Sprint 5.18b self-validation gates kept: --help / -h prints specific usage (and now documents wallet-free behaviour), missing arg / extra args / empty / odd-length / non-hex rejected with rc=1. Python broadcast guard gains a new broadcast_result_status enum value `cli_rejected` to distinguish CLI-side rejection (wallet load failure, hex validation, RPC auth) from node-side rejection (insufficient fee, double spend, mempool full); classification is by stderr substring against `_CLI_REJECTION_PATTERNS`. Receipt is still written for every subprocess attempt regardless of outcome. New tests: `test_sendrawtransaction_does_not_load_wallet` and `test_sendrawtransaction_help_does_not_load_wallet` confirm the wallet-free behaviour against the real binary (skip when sost-cli is missing or pre-5.18). `test_cli_rejected_wallet_load_failure` is a regression of the exact VPS stderr from the failed attempt. `test_classify_subprocess_failure` is a parametrised matrix over CLI vs node patterns. Funds untouched: every Trinity broadcast attempt to date has produced exactly one audit receipt and zero on-chain transactions. No consensus, RPC server, wallet, signer or wire-format changes.
